page: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server includes 5669 GB of local SSD storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. In benchmark evaluations, the D96ds_v7 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, achieving a top-tier Passmark CPU score of 106,546.32 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 97,236.44. Memory performance is highlighted by a top-tier read-only small block cached bandwidth of 7,269.34 GB/sec, alongside strong uncached large block read and write speeds.

The hardware configuration delivers top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving throughput at 151.5 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations reaching 156.15 million operations per second. It also exhibits strong capabilities in CPU-based LLM inference, particularly for prompt processing on medium and large models. The combination of high-capacity local storage, balanced memory-to-core ratio, and dedicated compute resources makes this instance highly cost-efficient for resource-dense enterprise workloads, large-scale databases, web serving, and parallel data processing without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 384.0 GB of RAM and 5669 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage compatibility. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmark testing, the server exhibits top-tier multi-core performance, outstanding memory bandwidth, and exceptional database operations throughput. It is highly optimized for enterprise databases, high-throughput static web serving, Redis deployments, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon virtual machine with 384 GB RAM and 5669 GB local storage, optimized for databases and web serving.
tagline: A high-capacity, general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, extensive local storage, and top-tier multi-core performance.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at 4.2 GHz with 384 GB RAM
5669 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and database operations throughput
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Suited for enterprise databases, Redis, and high-throughput web serving
